compiled_state.title: TOML loader + upward/XDG discovery in config.py
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
Add TOML reading ([tool.rebar] in pyproject.toml + standalone rebar.toml) via stdlib tomllib, with upward project discovery (stop at rebar.toml/[tool.rebar]/.git/fs-root) and a hand-rolled XDG user fallback ($XDG_CONFIG_HOME/~/.config/rebar) — NO platformdirs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tomllib-based loader for [tool.rebar] + rebar.toml in src/rebar/config.py
- [ ] Upward discovery + XDG user fallback; precedence project>user
- [ ] Tests for discovery order + precedence
Blocked by the typed-Config task; serialize with the route-reads task (shared config.py).

comments.body: DESIGN REQUIREMENT (from 252e subagent review): Config.from_mapping bakes defaults eagerly, so it CANNOT distinguish 'key absent' from 'key set to its default' — using it per-layer would let a lower-priority layer's default silently override a higher layer's explicit value. The loader MUST: (1) parse each layer (CLI/env/project/user) to a SPARSE coerced dict of ONLY-present keys (split coerce-and-validate from default-application); (2) deep-merge the sparse layers by precedence (CLI > env > project > user); (3) construct the typed Config (applying defaults) ONCE at the end via from_mapping on the merged dict. Add a sparse parse helper alongside the existing coercers; from_mapping stays the final merged-dict->typed step (already correct).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: DONE: TOML loader + discovery + layering in src/rebar/config.py. Schema-driven coerce_sparse (only-present keys, no defaults) + merge_sparse (later-wins per key) + from_mapping applies defaults ONCE — so a lower layer's default never clobbers a higher layer's explicit value (the sparse-merge property). load_config layers defaults<user(XDG)<project(rebar.toml/[tool.rebar]/legacy .rebar.conf)<env(REBAR_<SECT>_<KEY>)<cli. Upward discovery stops at .git/fs-root; $REBAR_CONFIG short-circuits; malformed/unreadable -> ConfigError (fail-closed). Hand-rolled XDG (no platformdirs). 20 loader tests covering every location, all precedence pairs, partial-section cross-layer merge, upward discovery, .git boundary, portability (identical across roots/cwd), unknown-warn, fail-closed + the 4 review-flagged combos. Subagent-reviewed: solid, no bugs. Unit tier green (247 passed). Perf no-caching note -> e211.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
